steps.title: Track the price advantage
steps.content: Imported goods may reduce expenses for the average consumer. For consumers far from ports, however, high transport costs could erase those price advantages.

steps.title: Choose the economic effect
steps.content: Choice B means criticize. Choice C means judge too low, and Choice D means misunderstand. Those words describe evaluation or interpretation, not the elimination of a price benefit by added costs. The answer is A.
